article.description: Deandre Moore hit two free throws with 3.3 seconds remaining as San Diego State beat UNLV 78-75 Saturday night to win the Mountain West Conference tournament and advance to the NCAA tournament for the first time since 1985.
article.headline: Long drought ends for tourney-bound Aztecs
article.linkText: Aztecs going to NCAAs for first time since '85
article.story: LAS VEGAS (AP) -- After losing twice to the Rebels in overtime during the regular season, San Diego State was finally able to beat Nevada-Las Vegas when it counted. Deandre Moore hit two free throws with 3.3 seconds remaining as the Aztecs beat UNLV 78-75 Saturday night to win the Mountain West Conference tournament and advance to the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 1985. "We hope this is the first of many championships here," said coach Steve Fisher, who as Michigan coach won one NCAA championship and played in two other finals. "We had a group of guys that competed hard. In the last 10 games, these are the two best teams in the conference. It's a tremendous accomplishment for San Diego State and our basketball team." The Aztecs led by as many as 16 points in the second half, but UNLV closed to 76-75 on two free throws by Marcus Banks with 4.8 seconds remaining. But Moore was fouled and converted both free throws for the Aztecs (21-11). The game was reminiscent of the two teams' last game in Las Vegas, when the Rebels overcame a late double-digit lead and eventually won 83-76 in overtime. "The last time we were here with 5:57 to go, they scored 21 points and (point guard Marcus) Banks scored 19 of them," Fisher said. "We had to stop him from going to the rim. We had to make it hard on him." UNLV cut the lead to 76-75 on two Banks free throws with 4.8 seconds remaining. Moore was fouled and converted both free throws to put the Aztecs (21-11) up by three. After a timeout, the Rebel's Lou Kelly had a shot from just inside of halfcourt that hit the back of the rim and bounced long. "They made me make a spin move, once I got lined up I thought it was going in," Kelly said. "In the first half, I was too excited. In the second half I relaxed. I got the ball and went to get it as close as I can." Tournament most valuable player Randy Holcomb led San Diego State with 20 points and 12 rebounds. Al Faux added 16 points and Mike Mackell 15. "It's like a dream come true, we're finally going to the tournament," said Aztec senior Myron Epps, the only four-year San Diego State letterman. Kelly led the Rebels (20-10) with 24 points. Dalron Johnson had 23 points and Banks scored 18. Despite the defeat, the Rebels are still holding out for a possible NCAA Tournament at-large bid, but coach Charlie Spoonhour wasn't too confident. "I would love to have an NCAA bid," said Spoonhour, who has previously led Saint Louis University and Southwest Missouri State into the tournament. "Wyoming won the league and should be favored to get in. I would love to get in, but I've had some good experience in the NIT and it's good for the seniors to keep playing." The Aztecs seemed to have the game in hand, leading from the 16:13 mark of the first half. San Diego State led 68-53 with five minutes remaining, but turned the ball over five consecutive times as the Rebels rallied to cut the lead to 68-62 with 3:55 left. Banks hit a 3-pointer and two field goals, and Johnson hit two free throws in that run. Mackell ended the run with a layup to put the Aztecs ahead 70-62. Banks again cut the lead to six with two free throws and after another Aztecs turnover Johnson scored to make it 70-66. Faux drove to the basket for a layup to put the Aztecs up 72-66, but Johnson answered with another basket. The Aztecs extended their lead to 74-68 on two Moore free throws, but Banks made a 3-point play to bring the Rebels to 74-71 with 1:06 remaining. Tony Bland made one of two free throws for San Diego State with 34.6 seconds remaining, but Kelly countered with an inside jumper to pull the Rebels to 75-73 with 30 seconds left. Faux was fouled and made one of two free throws with 25.8 seconds left and after an exchange of turnovers, Banks made two free throws bring the Rebels to 76-75 with 4.8 seconds left. Moore was immediately fouled, but sank his free throws to seal the victory.
